scope.summary: Implement a hierarchical Tailor Scene Object Browser panel listing all garments, avatars, trims, and layer ordering for a workspace session with per-item visibility toggle and stable element IDs
acceptance_criteria: GET /tailor/scene returns the full scene object tree for the active workspace session: { avatars: [...], garments: [{ id, name, status, layer_order, visible, trims: [...] }] }
The frontend SceneObjectBrowser renders the tree with expand/collapse per garment and per trim, each with a visibility toggle checkbox
Toggling visibility for a garment or trim in the browser updates the viewport render without a full re-simulation
Every tree node has a stable data-testid element ID keyed to its entity ID for parallel-model interaction (HBR-VIS)
A model can call GET /tailor/scene to enumerate all scene entities and their layer order without a GUI session
Selecting an item in the scene browser sets the active selection context (same as clicking in the viewport)
The browser updates in real time when a garment is added, promoted, or removed during the session
Headless capture of the scene browser in populated and empty states passes the capture matrix gate (MT-244)
proof_targets: HBR-VIS: headless capture of SceneObjectBrowser with 3 garments + 2 avatars + trims passes screenshot gate
scope.risk_if_missed: Without a scene object tree, multi-garment scenes with layered garments and multiple avatars have no organized navigation surface; operators and models must use flat ID lists from the REST API, making complex layered outfit management impractical and diverging from the MD workflow where the Object Browser is the primary scene navigation tool.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
